message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: I own streaming_listener.py for a debug-logging feature (opt-in trace logs). Please tell me which files you own so we can keep our edits disjoint.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/messages.py,dspy/streaming/streamify.py
note: I'm implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in StreamListener (streaming_listener.py). Feature1 (is_last_chunk) sounds like it may live in messages.py/streamify.py - proposing you own those so we don't collide. Let me know if you need to touch streaming_listener.py too.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need streaming_listener.py (mine: idle_timeout_s param + self._last_chunk_ts + new tick() method; yours: debug logging). Since it's unavoidable, let's keep edits additive and non-overlapping in the same file: I'll only touch __init__ (add new kwarg/attr), end of receive() (update _last_chunk_ts), and add a new tick() method at the end of the class. Please keep your logging changes to log statements inserted in existing lines/methods (receive/flush) rather than restructuring them, so git merge can auto-combine. I'll commit+push my version now; feel free to merge on top or work independently - we'll reconcile via git merge before final patch. OK to both proceed?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: My feature (debug trace-logging) is spec'd to land in streaming_listener.py too - it adds debug/debug_logger params to __init__ and guarded DEBUG log calls inside existing methods (start detection, end detection, emit chunk, flush, reset). I won't touch tick()/idle_timeout logic or add new methods. Suggest: you add idle_timeout_s/tick() as new/separate code (e.g. new method + its own __init__ kwargs), I'll add debug/debug_logger as separate __init__ kwargs + log lines inline in existing methods. We each stick to our own added lines so git merge should apply cleanly. OK to proceed in parallel on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed: I'll add idle_timeout_s/self._last_chunk_ts as new __init__ kwargs/attrs, update end of receive() with a minimal timestamp-refresh line, and add a new tick() method. Won't touch your debug/debug_logger kwargs or your inline log lines. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Accepted - proceeding now. I'll only add __init__ kwargs (debug, debug_logger) + self._logger, and insert guarded DEBUG log lines inline into existing methods (no restructuring, no renames). Will reconcile via git merge before final patch.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished: added idle_timeout_s kwarg, self._last_chunk_ts tracking updated at end of the chunk-parsing try/except in receive(), and a new tick(now_ts=None) method placed between receive() and flush(). No changes to your debug/logging additions. Tests in tests/streaming pass. Committing now and will merge your branch before final patch.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Finished debug trace-logging feature. Committed and pushed to agent2 branch. All log formats verified against spec examples exactly, tests/streaming pass. Ready to merge your branch now.
